text: Morning shift at the coffee shop. A customer left a $10 tip on a $5 drink and said 'keep being creative.' I thought about it all day.
Wondering if Austin is where I want to stay long-term. Cost of living is creeping up. But the community here is real.
Skated to Barton Springs with roommates. Didn't think about work for four hours. Rare and necessary.
Used Claude to draft three proposal emails in 20 minutes. Sent all three. That would've taken me half a day of avoidance otherwise.
Spent Sunday learning Blender. Made a donut. It looks exactly like the tutorial donut. Starting somewhere.
Pulled an all-nighter finishing a brand identity project I quoted too low. Delivered it. Client loved it. Charged $600. Should've been $2,200.
Went to a design meetup downtown. Knew nobody. Awkward for the first hour. Left with two business cards and one real conversation.
Client asked for 'just a few changes' on a logo. 12 rounds of revisions later I want to retire. I need a revision policy. I keep saying I'll write one.
Looked at my credit card balance: $5,840. Paid the minimum. Hate that I paid the minimum.
Finished a rebrand project I'm actually proud of. Put it on Instagram. Seven designers I follow liked it. One commented. I screenshotted it.
Tried to do taxes. Got overwhelmed. Closed the tab. Opened it again. Got through the first form. Victory.
I think my ADHD is actually kind of useful for creative work. The hyperfocus when I find the right brief is unlike anything. It's the boring admin that wrecks me.
Opened Figma to work on a personal project. Fell into a rabbit hole of font comparisons for two hours. Did not open the project.
Portfolio website hit 1,000 unique visitors in a month. First time. Mostly organic from an Instagram post that randomly did numbers.
Long bike ride through East Austin. The murals on 6th are unreal. Made a note to reach out to the building owners about a collab.
One of my roommates got a 'real job' at a startup. Base salary, health insurance, 401k. We celebrated. I felt proud and also weird about it.
Got referred to a new client by someone I did work for eight months ago. First time a referral has led directly to a contract. This is how it's supposed to work.
Wondering if Austin is where I want to stay long-term. Cost of living is creeping up. But the community here is real.

First month where freelance income covered all my expenses without the coffee shop shifts. Small but monumental.
Used Claude to draft three proposal emails in 20 minutes. Sent all three. That would've taken me half a day of avoidance otherwise.
Spent an afternoon at the UT library working. Something about being around students makes me focus. Borrowed their energy.
Spent Sunday learning Blender. Made a donut. It looks exactly like the tutorial donut. Starting somewhere.
Raised my rate for the first time. New clients: $85/hour. Old rate was $55. Said it out loud for the first time on a call and didn't die.
